#### Stage 4: Implement handleItemClick & Apply Color Coding to Rendering ✅ COMPLETE
**Task**: Create handleItemClick function with re-selection behavior and apply color coding to all rendered items
**Specific Actions**:
- Create `handleItemClick` helper function in MainMenu.tsx (after getSelectionColor):
  - Input: `item: Category | Subcategory | ContentItem, itemType: 'category' | 'subcategory' | 'content'`
  - Re-selection check (logic doc line 152, tech-ref lines 2721-2729):
    - If `pageState !== 'collapsed-reader'`:
      - Check if item already selected (compare IDs with selectedCategory/selectedSubcategory/selectedContent)
      - Return early if already selected (no change)
  - Call appropriate callback based on itemType:
    - `itemType === 'category'` → `onCategorySelect(item as Category)`
    - `itemType === 'subcategory'` → `onSubcategorySelect(item as Subcategory)`
    - `itemType === 'content'` → `onContentSelect(item as ContentItem)`

  - Add `onClick={() => handleItemClick(cat, 'category')}` to div (line 97)
  - Replace static `text-[#6b7280]` with dynamic color: `${getSelectionColor(cat.id, 'category') === 'green' ? 'text-[#00ff88]' : 'text-[#6b7280]'}` per tech-ref lines 3844-3846
  - Keep `hover:text-[#e5e7eb]` class per tech-ref line 3846
- Update Column 2 rendering (subcategories):
  - Add `onClick={() => handleItemClick(sub, 'subcategory')}` to div (line 113)
  - Replace static `text-[#6b7280]` with dynamic color using getSelectionColor
- Update Column 3 rendering (content):
  - Add `onClick={() => handleItemClick(item, 'content')}` to div (line 129)
  - Replace static `text-[#6b7280]` with dynamic color using getSelectionColor

## PHASE 4: CONTENT DISPLAY SYSTEM

**Purpose**: Display selected content with all metadata in Content Reader and Info Menu

**Why This Phase Exists**: Content display is the core user-facing feature. Requires working Main menu selection (Phase 3) before content can be shown. Implements rendering for all 4 content types (article, image, video, audio) and displays complete metadata per logic doc specification.

**Phase Result**: Content Reader component displays selected content with correct positioning for both expanded and collapsed states; all 4 content types render correctly (article with EditorJS, image/video/audio with appropriate players); Info Menu displays complete metadata with byline/link style labels in green
**Phase Status**: ✅ COMPLETE

**Phase 3 Foundation Summary (for Phase 4 reference)**:
- **MainMenu Component**: Fully functional at `components/tabs/MainMenu.tsx` (279 lines total)
- **Three-Column Layout**: Categories → Subcategories → Content Items with hierarchical filtering
- **Selection Logic**: All selection handlers implemented in `PortfolioTab.tsx`:
  - `handleCategorySelect` - Sets category, clears subcategory/content, sets `pageState` to `expanded-empty`
  - `handleSubcategorySelect` - Sets subcategory, clears content, sets `pageState` to `expanded-empty`
  - `handleContentSelect` - Sets content, sets `pageState` to `collapsed-reader` (Rule 3)
  - `handleMainMenuClick` - Expands from `collapsed-reader` to `expanded-reader` without changing selection (Rule 4)
- **State Management**: `selectedContent` state in `PortfolioTab.tsx` available for Content Reader
- **Page States**: All three states functional (`expanded-empty`, `expanded-reader`, `collapsed-reader`)
- **Collapsed State**: Horizontal breadcrumb format with separators (Category | Subcategory | Content)
- **Expanded State**: Three-column view with all items displayed and color coding

**Phase Dependencies**:
```
Step 4.1 (Content Reader Structure) → Step 4.2 (Title/Subtitle) → 
Step 4.3 (Article Rendering) → Step 4.4 (Other Content Types) → 
Step 4.5 (Info Menu Structure) → Step 4.6 (Metadata Display)
```

**Architecture Note**: Step 4.4 implementation depends on Step 0.3 decision (ContentViewer reuse vs custom build)

---

  - Implement conditional: `{content.type === 'video' && content.video_url && (<div className="my-8">{content.video_url.includes('youtube.com') || content.video_url.includes('youtu.be') ? (<div className="aspect-video"><iframe src={content.video_url} className="w-full h-full rounded-lg" allowFullScreen /></div>) : (<video src={content.video_url} controls className="w-full rounded-lg" />)}</div>)}` per ContentViewer.tsx pattern (lines 221-239) and tech-ref lines 2607-2612
  - YouTube detection: Check if video_url includes 'youtube.com' or 'youtu.be' to determine iframe vs HTML5 video
  - Iframe for YouTube: Use aspect-video container with iframe element, allowFullScreen attribute
  - HTML5 video for other URLs: Use video element with controls attribute
  - Position rendering inside content div after image content (maintains container structure)
  - Add comment: `{/* Video content - Step 4.4 Stage 2 */}` indicating Step 4.4 implementation
  - Note: Only renders when content.type === 'video' AND content.video_url exists (null check prevents errors)
- **Testing Checkpoint**: ✅ Video content renders when content.type === 'video', YouTube URLs display as iframe, non-YouTube URLs display as HTML5 video player, no rendering when content is not video type, no errors when video_url is null, video players display with proper styling and controls
- **Result**: Video type conditional rendering implemented, YouTube iframe detection works correctly, HTML5 video player displays for non-YouTube URLs, ready for Stage 3 audio type rendering

#### Stage 2: Add Null & Empty String Handling ✅ COMPLETE
- **Task**: Handle null and empty string values gracefully for all metadata fields
- **Specific Actions**:
  - ✅ Line 1: Handle null/empty publication_name and publication_date (InfoMenu.tsx lines 46-60)
    - Added conditional rendering: `{(metadata.publication_name || metadata.publication_date) && (...)}` to hide line if both null/empty
    - If publication_name exists: Display in green, show " / " separator only if publication_date also exists
    - If publication_date exists: Display in gray (with or without publication_name)

  - ✅ Line 2: Handle null/empty byline_style_text and author_name (InfoMenu.tsx lines 62-76)
    - Added conditional rendering: `{(metadata.byline_style_text || metadata.author_name) && (...)}` to hide line if both null/empty
    - If byline_style_text exists: Display in green, show ": " separator only if author_name also exists
    - If author_name exists: Display in gray (with or without byline_style_text)
    - Format handles all cases: both fields, only byline_style_text, only author_name, neither (line hidden)
  - ✅ Line 3: Handle null/empty link_style_text and source_link (InfoMenu.tsx lines 78-99)
    - Added conditional rendering: `{(metadata.link_style_text || metadata.source_link) && (...)}` to hide line if both null/empty
    - If link_style_text exists: Display in green, show ": " separator only if source_link also exists
    - If source_link exists: Render as clickable link with hover effect, target="_blank", rel="noopener noreferrer"
    - If source_link is null/empty but link_style_text exists: Display "—" placeholder in gray
    - Link element only renders if source_link is truthy (valid URL)
  - ✅ Conditional rendering added to hide entire lines if all fields are null/empty
  - ✅ Link element only renders if source_link is valid (truthy check)
- **Testing Checkpoint**: ✅ Null publication_name handled gracefully (line shows only publication_date), null publication_date handled gracefully (line shows only publication_name), null byline_style_text handled gracefully (line shows only author_name), null author_name handled gracefully (line shows only byline_style_text), null link_style_text handled gracefully (line shows only source_link as link), null source_link handled gracefully (line shows link_style_text with "—" placeholder), empty strings handled correctly (treated same as null), lines hidden when all fields null (conditional rendering prevents rendering), link only renders for valid URLs (source_link truthy check)
- **Result**: Null and empty string handling implemented for all metadata fields, InfoMenu displays gracefully with missing data, separators only show when both fields present, link element only renders for valid URLs, ready for Stage 3 final verification

## PHASE 5: COLLECTIONS SYSTEM

**Purpose**: Implement Collections Menu and collection tab filtering functionality

**Why This Phase Exists**: Collections system is a major feature layering on top of existing Main menu and Content Reader. Requires both to be fully functional before implementing filtered views. Adds dynamic collection tabs to BottomTabBar established in Phase 2.

**Phase Result**: Collections Menu displays correctly with featured/linked visibility rules; clicking collection opens filtered tab in BottomTabBar; Main menu filters to show only collection content; collection tabs support multiple open collections; close button returns to main Portfolio view; all collection filtering and state management functional

**Phase Dependencies**:
```
Step 5.1 (Collections Menu Structure) → Step 5.2 (Visibility Rules) → 
Step 5.3 (Collection Click) → Step 5.4 (Filtering Logic) → 
Step 5.5 (Content Selection) → Step 5.6 (Close Mechanism) → 
Step 5.7 (Multiple Collections)
```

---

### Step 5.2: Collections Menu - Visibility Rules
**Status**: ✅ COMPLETE

**Purpose**: Implement featured and linked collections visibility logic

### Step 5.4: Collection Tab - Filtering Logic
**Status**: ✅ COMPLETE
**Prerequisites**: Step 5.3 complete (collection tab opens)
**Purpose**: Filter Main menu to show only content items in selected collection
**Result**: Content query joins content_collections table and filters by collection_id; Main menu displays only content items linked to collection; categories filter to show only if they contain at least one content item in collection; subcategories filter to show only if they contain at least one content item in collection; same featured/byline_style/link_style validation still applied; categories and subcategories reused from main database (not separate per collection)
**Logic Doc Reference**: Lines 216-223 (collection tab filtering), lines 106-107 (main menu filtered), lines 218 (query logic)
**Tech-Ref Lines**: 4375-4470, 3531-3564, 4429-4469, 871-876, 1516-1577 — Collection content filtering (filterContentByCollection/filterCategoriesByCollection/filterSubcategoriesByCollection functions), categories in collection tabs filter (cascading filter showing only categories with collection content), usage in state useMemo (displayedCategories/Subcategories/Content with collection filtering), CollectionFilteringResult type (filtered content/categories/subcategories structure), Test Set 3 collection-content links for testing filtering
**Testing**: Collection tab shows filtered Main menu; only collection content appears in column 3; categories/subcategories without collection content hidden; featured validation still enforced; console shows filtered query results
**Stages**:

#### Stage 1: Create Collection Filtering Helper Functions ✅ COMPLETE
- **Task**: Create helper functions to filter content, categories, and subcategories by collection
- **Specific Actions**:
  - Create `filterContentByCollection` function in PortfolioTab.tsx:
    - Parameters: `content: ContentItem[]`, `collectionSlug: string`
    - Logic: Filter content items where `collection_slugs` array includes `collectionSlug`
    - Return: `ContentItem[]` (filtered content items)
    - Handle edge case: If `collection_slugs` is undefined/null, return empty array
  - Create `filterCategoriesByCollection` function:
    - Parameters: `categories: Category[]`, `subcategories: Subcategory[]`, `filteredContent: ContentItem[]`
    - Logic: Filter categories that contain at least one subcategory that has filtered content
    - Cascading filter: Category shows only if at least one of its subcategories has collection content
    - Return: `Category[]` (filtered categories)
  - Create `filterSubcategoriesByCollection` function:
    - Parameters: `subcategories: Subcategory[]`, `filteredContent: ContentItem[]`
    - Logic: Filter subcategories that contain at least one filtered content item
    - Return: `Subcategory[]` (filtered subcategories)
  - Add helper function to check if activeTab is a collection:
    - Create `isCollectionTab` helper: `(activeTab: string, activeCollections: CollectionTab[]) => boolean`
    - Returns `true` if `activeTab` matches a collection slug in `activeCollections`
    - Returns `false` if `activeTab` is 'portfolio', 'resume', or 'downloads'
  - Add helper function to get current collection slug:
    - Create `getCurrentCollectionSlug` helper: `(activeTab: string, activeCollections: CollectionTab[]) => string | null`
    - Returns collection slug if `activeTab` is a collection, `null` otherwise
- **Testing Checkpoint**: ✅ Helper functions created, filterContentByCollection filters by collection_slugs array, filterCategoriesByCollection cascades correctly, filterSubcategoriesByCollection filters correctly, isCollectionTab and getCurrentCollectionSlug helpers work correctly, no TypeScript errors
- **Result**: Collection filtering helper functions ready for use in useMemo hooks
- **Positioning**: Add helper functions after data transformation functions (around line 410 in PortfolioTab.tsx)

#### Stage 2: Create useMemo Hooks for Filtered Data ✅ COMPLETE
- **Task**: Create useMemo hooks to compute filtered content, categories, and subcategories when collection tab is active
- **Specific Actions**:
  - Create `filteredContent` useMemo hook:
    - Dependencies: `[content, activeTab, activeCollections]`
    - Logic: If `isCollectionTab(activeTab, activeCollections)` is true:
      - Get collection slug: `getCurrentCollectionSlug(activeTab, activeCollections)`
      - Filter content: `filterContentByCollection(content, collectionSlug)`
    - Else: Return full `content` array (no filtering for main tabs)
    - Return: `ContentItem[]`
  - Create `filteredCategories` useMemo hook:
    - Dependencies: `[categories, subcategories, filteredContent]`
    - Logic: If collection tab active:
      - Filter categories: `filterCategoriesByCollection(categories, subcategories, filteredContent)`
    - Else: Return full `categories` array
    - Return: `Category[]`
  - Create `filteredSubcategories` useMemo hook:
    - Dependencies: `[subcategories, filteredContent]`
    - Logic: If collection tab active:
      - Filter subcategories: `filterSubcategoriesByCollection(subcategories, filteredContent)`
    - Else: Return full `subcategories` array
    - Return: `Subcategory[]`
  - Add comments explaining filtering logic and when it applies
- **Testing Checkpoint**: ✅ useMemo hooks created, filteredContent filters correctly when collection tab active, filteredCategories cascades correctly, filteredSubcategories filters correctly, full arrays returned when main tab active, no TypeScript errors
- **Result**: Filtered data computed correctly based on activeTab state
- **Positioning**: Add useMemo hooks after featuredCollections useMemo (around line 143 in PortfolioTab.tsx)

### Step 5.5: Collection Tab - Content Selection & Display
**Status**: ✅ COMPLETE
**Prerequisites**: Step 5.4 complete (filtering working)
**Purpose**: Implement automatic content selection when collection tab opens
**Result**: Collection tab loads in expanded-reader state; if current content item is in collection, it remains selected and displays in Content Reader; if current content item NOT in collection, first content item in collection (by order_index) auto-selects and displays; Content Reader updates to show selected collection content
**Logic Doc Reference**: Lines 103-105 (content selection logic), line 103 (loads in expanded-reader)
**Tech-Ref Lines**: 3322-3337, 4232-4288, 1169-1192, 1787-1802, 3689-3725 — Rule 5 edge case (keep selection if current content in collection, else select first by order_index), auto-selection logic reusable (autoSelectInitialContent pattern applicable to collection tabs), Collection tab state types (CollectionTabState/CollectionTabLoadBehavior), Test Set 6 Scenario 4 collection tab expected auto-selection behavior, state persistence patterns (collectionSelections Record tracking last content per collection)

**Stages**:

#### Stage 1: Create Helper Function for Collection Tab Content Selection ✅ COMPLETE
- **Task**: Create helper function to auto-select content when collection tab opens
- **Specific Actions**:
  - Create `autoSelectCollectionContent` function in PortfolioTab.tsx:
    - Parameters: `filteredContent: ContentItem[]`, `currentContent: ContentItem | null`, `collectionSlug: string`, `filteredCategories: Category[]`, `filteredSubcategories: Subcategory[]`
    - Logic: Check if `currentContent` is in collection:
      - Check if `currentContent?.collection_slugs` includes `collectionSlug`
      - If yes: Return current content with its category/subcategory (find category/subcategory from filtered arrays)
      - If no: Select first content item in `filteredContent` by `order_index` (sort by order_index ascending, take first)
    - Return: `{ selectedCategory: Category | null, selectedSubcategory: Subcategory | null, selectedContent: ContentItem | null }`
    - Handle edge cases: Empty filteredContent returns all null, missing category/subcategory returns null for those fields
  - Add helper function to find category/subcategory for a content item:
    - Create `findCategorySubcategoryForContent` helper: `(content: ContentItem, categories: Category[], subcategories: Subcategory[]) => { category: Category | null, subcategory: Subcategory | null }`
    - Logic: Find subcategory by `content.subcategory_id`, then find category by `subcategory.category_id`
    - Return: Category and subcategory objects or null if not found
  - Add comments explaining selection logic per logic doc lines 103-105
- **Testing Checkpoint**: ✅ Helper function created, autoSelectCollectionContent checks if current content in collection correctly, selects first content by order_index if current not in collection, finds category/subcategory correctly, handles edge cases correctly, no TypeScript errors

    - Remove collection from `activeCollections` array using `setActiveCollections(prev => prev.filter(c => c.slug !== slug))`
    - Check if closed collection is currently active tab: `if (activeTab === slug)`
    - If closing current tab: Switch `activeTab` to 'portfolio' using `setActiveTab('portfolio')`
    - If closing non-active tab: Leave `activeTab` unchanged (edge case per logic doc line 418)
  - Add comments explaining the logic per tech-ref Rule 9 (lines 3742-3754)
  - Remove placeholder console.log and comment
- **Positioning**: Replace placeholder function at lines 832-836
- **Testing Checkpoint**: ✅ Function implemented correctly, removes collection from array, switches activeTab if closing current tab, leaves activeTab unchanged if closing non-active tab, no TypeScript errors
- **Result**: `handleCollectionClose` function fully functional, removes collection from activeCollections array, switches activeTab to 'portfolio' when closing current tab

#### Stage 2: Test Collection Tab Closing Behavior ✅ COMPLETE

## PHASE 6: LAYOUT FRAME (Layout Only - No New Logic)

**Purpose**: Arrange all existing components on the page so they sit in the correct places and scroll correctly. This phase only changes where things appear on screen, not how they work.

**Why This Phase Exists**: By the end of Phase 5, all components (Profile, Main Menu, Content Reader, Info Menu, Collections Menu, Bottom Tab Bar) will be built and working, but they may not be positioned correctly on the page. This phase fixes their positions so nothing overlaps and everything scrolls properly.

**Phase Result**: All components positioned correctly on the page; Profile stays at the top when scrolling; Menu Bar (Main Menu + Collections Menu) sticks right under Profile; Content Reader sits in the center and scrolls properly; Info Menu stays in place while Content Reader scrolls; Bottom Tab Bar stays at the bottom; nothing overlaps incorrectly; layout works in both collapsed and expanded states; layout works on different window sizes and shapes.

**Important Note**: This phase does NOT add any new features or logic. It only moves and positions components that already exist. All the clicking, selecting, filtering, and displaying logic stays exactly the same.

**Implementation Strategy**: Build collapsed state first (simpler layout), then add expanded state (more complex). This allows us to establish the foundation and get Content Reader positioning working correctly before adding the complexity of expanded menus.

**Phase Dependencies**:
```
PART 1: COLLAPSED STATE (Steps 1-5)
Step 6.1 (Page Frame Structure) → Step 6.2 (Content Reader - Collapsed State) → 
Step 6.3 (Menu Bar - Collapsed State) → Step 6.4 (Info Menu - Collapsed State) → 
Step 6.5 (Collapsed State Testing)

PART 2: EXPANDED STATE (Steps 6-10)
Step 6.6 (Menu Bar - Expanded State) → Step 6.7 (Content Reader - Expanded State) → 
Step 6.8 (Info Menu Visibility - Expanded State) → Step 6.9 (State Transitions Testing) → 
Step 6.10 (Final Refinements & Window Size Testing)
```

---

### Step 6.1: Page Frame Structure
**Status**: ✅ COMPLETE

**Purpose**: Create the basic page structure that holds all components in the right order
**Result**: PortfolioTab component has a clear structure with separate areas for: Profile at top, Menu Bar area (Main Menu + Collections Menu), Content Reader area, Info Menu area, and Bottom Tab Bar at bottom. The page uses a simple vertical stacking layout (one thing above another) without complex positioning formulas. Profile height measurement is working and available for Menu Bar positioning.
**What This Means in Plain Language**: Set up the page like stacking boxes - Profile box on top, then Menu Bar box (contains both Main Menu and Collections Menu), then Content Reader box in the middle, Info Menu box on the left, and Bottom Tab Bar box at the bottom.
**Testing**: Page loads with all areas visible; no components are missing; basic structure is clear in browser DevTools; Profile height measurement working

#### Stage 2: Update Info Menu Vertical Positioning
- **Task**: Change Info Menu vertical position to be centered between Menu Bar and Bottom Tab Bar (per layout-reset.md lines 355-357)
- **Specific Actions**:
  - Calculate vertical center position:
    - Menu Bar bottom: `profileHeight + 25px + 64px` (Profile height + gap + Menu Bar height)
    - Bottom Tab Bar top: `100vh - 64px` (viewport height - Bottom Tab Bar height)
    - Center point: `(Menu Bar bottom + Bottom Tab Bar top) / 2`
    - Use `transform: translateY(-50%)` to center vertically
  - Update InfoMenu component (`components/InfoMenu.tsx` lines 38-44):
    - Change `top: '75%'` to calculated center position
    - Keep `transform: 'translateY(-50%)'` for vertical centering
    - Update comment: "Vertical: Middle of center plane (excluding Profile and Bottom Tab Bar), vertically centered in available space between Menu Bar and Bottom Tab Bar (per layout-reset.md lines 355-357)"
  - Alternative approach (simpler):
    - Use `top: '50%'` with `transform: translateY(-50%)` to center in viewport
    - Adjust with offset to account for Menu Bar and Bottom Tab Bar
    - Or use `calc()` to calculate: `top: calc((100vh - 64px - (profileHeight + 25px + 64px)) / 2 + profileHeight + 25px + 64px)`
  - **Recommended**: Use viewport-based calculation with Profile height and Bottom Tab Bar height
- **Positioning**: Update vertical position to center between Menu Bar and Bottom Tab Bar
- **Testing Checkpoint**: Info Menu vertically centered in available space; lower than Content Reader top border; centered between Menu Bar and Bottom Tab Bar; ready for Stage 3 (visibility)
- **Result**: ✅ COMPLETE - Info Menu vertically positioned correctly, centered between Menu Bar and Bottom Tab Bar
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **Files Modified**: `components/InfoMenu.tsx`, `components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx`
  - **InfoMenu.tsx Changes** (lines 20-24, 26, 32-50):
    - Updated `InfoMenuProps` interface to include `profileHeight?: number` (optional prop for vertical centering calculation)
    - Updated function signature to accept `profileHeight = 0` with default value
    - Added vertical positioning calculation (lines 33-40):
      - Menu Bar bottom: `profileHeight + 25px + 64px` (Profile height + gap + Menu Bar height)
      - Bottom Tab Bar top: `100vh - 64px` (viewport height - Bottom Tab Bar height)
      - Center point: `calc((profileHeight + 25px + 100vh) / 2)` using CSS calc()
      - Fallback: `calc((200px + 25px + 100vh) / 2)` if profileHeight not set
    - Updated `top` style from `'75%'` placeholder to calculated `centerPoint` (line 48)
    - Updated comment (line 43): "Vertical: Middle of center plane (excluding Profile and Bottom Tab Bar), vertically centered in available space between Menu Bar and Bottom Tab Bar (per layout-reset.md lines 355-357)"
    - Kept `transform: 'translateY(-50%)'` for vertical centering (line 50)
  - **PortfolioTab.tsx Changes** (line 968):
    - Added `profileHeight={profileHeight}` prop to InfoMenu component
    - Passes Profile height from state to InfoMenu for vertical centering calculation
  - **Positioning Logic**:
    - Vertical: Calculated center point between Menu Bar bottom and Bottom Tab Bar top
    - Uses CSS `calc()` for dynamic calculation based on Profile height
    - Falls back to approximate Profile height (200px) if not set
    - `transform: translateY(-50%)` centers Info Menu vertically relative to calculated position
    - Horizontal: Fixed at `25px` from left edge (unchanged from Stage 1)
- **Potential Issues**:
  - ⚠️ Vertical centering may be slightly off if Profile height changes after initial render (mitigated by reactive profileHeight prop)
  - ⚠️ Fallback calculation uses 200px approximate Profile height - may not be exact until Profile height is measured (expected behavior, will adjust when Profile height is set)
  - ⚠️ Info Menu may shift slightly during initial page load if Profile height not yet measured (expected behavior, will settle once Profile height is set)

    - **Option 2 (Simpler)**: Use estimated Info Menu width (e.g., 200-300px) and calculate `marginLeft = 25px + estimatedWidth + gap`
    - Update `marginLeft` calculation in ContentReader.tsx:
      - Change from: `marginLeft = positioning === 'collapsed' ? '0px' : '0px'`
      - To: `marginLeft = positioning === 'collapsed' ? 'calc(25px + [Info Menu width] + 20px)' : '0px'`

      - Updated comment: "Left margin: Info Menu left (25px) + Info Menu width (estimated 250px) + gap (20px) = 295px. Fills space between Info Menu right edge and right edge of page (per layout-reset.md line 322)"
      - Expanded state: `'0px'` (placeholder for Step 6.6)
  - **PortfolioTab.tsx Changes** (lines 950-971):
    - Separated ContentReader and InfoMenu rendering into separate conditionals
    - ContentReader: Visible in both `collapsed-reader` and `expanded-reader` states (unchanged)
    - InfoMenu: Updated visibility to only show in `collapsed-reader` state:
      - Changed from: `{(pageState === 'collapsed-reader' || pageState === 'expanded-reader') && (`
      - To: `{pageState === 'collapsed-reader' && (`
      - Updated comment: "InfoMenu - only visible in collapsed state (per layout-reset.md line 363)"
    - Removed fragment wrapper (`<>...</>`) since components are now in separate conditionals
  - **Positioning Logic**:
    - Content Reader left margin (collapsed): 295px (starts after Info Menu right edge)
    - Content Reader right margin (collapsed): 50px (gap from right edge)
    - Content Reader left margin (expanded): 0px (placeholder for Step 6.6)

### Step 6.6: Menu Bar - Expanded State
**Status**: NOT STARTED
**Prerequisites**: Step 6.4 complete (Info Menu positioned in collapsed state) - Step 6.5 cancelled due to expanded state visibility issue
**Purpose**: Position Menu Bar in expanded state. Main Menu expands to show three columns, Collections Menu expands to show vertical list.
**Result**: Menu Bar expands correctly. Main Menu (expanded three columns) grows rightward and downward from its left starting point (~60px from left). Collections Menu (expanded vertical list) grows leftward and downward from its right starting point (~60px from right). Both menus maintain their horizontal positions but expand vertically. Natural gap between menus widens/shrinks with window size. Menu Bar height becomes dynamic based on content.
**What This Means in Plain Language**: When menus expand, Main Menu shows three columns and grows down, Collections Menu shows vertical list and grows down. The gap between them adjusts with window size.
**Testing**: Menu Bar expands correctly; Main Menu shows three columns; Collections Menu shows vertical list; both grow downward correctly; gap between menus adjusts with window size; Menu Bar height is dynamic
**Stages**:

#### Stage 1: Update Menu Bar Container for Dynamic Height
- **Task**: Remove fixed height constraint and allow Menu Bar to grow vertically in expanded state
- **Specific Actions**:
  - Update Menu Bar Container (`components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx` lines 917-924):
    - Remove `h-16` (fixed 64px height) from className
    - Add `min-h-16` (minimum 64px height for collapsed state) to className
    - Keep `flex justify-between items-center` for horizontal layout
    - Keep `px-[60px]` for horizontal padding
    - Keep `fixed` positioning and `z-10 bg-[#1a1d23]`
    - Change `items-center` to `items-start` to allow vertical growth (menus align to top when expanded)
    - Update comment: "Menu Bar height: Dynamic in expanded state, minimum 64px in collapsed state (per layout-reset.md lines 293-302)"
  - Verify Menu Bar container allows vertical growth:
    - Container should expand to fit tallest menu (Main Menu or Collections Menu)
    - Both menus should be able to grow downward without being cut off
- **Positioning**: Update Menu Bar container to support dynamic height
- **Testing Checkpoint**: Menu Bar container allows vertical growth; minimum height 64px maintained in collapsed state; ready for Stage 2 (menu expansion verification)
- **Result**: ✅ COMPLETE - Menu Bar container updated to support dynamic height in expanded state
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **File Modified**: `components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx`
  - **Lines Changed**: 914-923 (Menu Bar Container className and comments)
  - **Changes Made**:
    - Removed `h-16` (fixed 64px height) from className (line 918)
    - Added `min-h-16` (minimum 64px height for collapsed state) to className (line 918)
    - Changed `items-center` to `items-start` to allow vertical growth (menus align to top when expanded) (line 918)
    - Updated comments (lines 914-917):
      - Added comment: "Menu Bar height: Dynamic in expanded state, minimum 64px in collapsed state (per layout-reset.md lines 293-302)"
      - Updated existing comments to reflect dynamic height behavior
    - Kept all other classes unchanged: `fixed z-10 bg-[#1a1d23] flex justify-between px-[60px]`
    - Kept all inline styles unchanged: `top`, `left`, `right` positioning
  - **Positioning Logic**:
    - **Height**: Dynamic (expands to fit tallest menu in expanded state), minimum 64px (collapsed state)
    - **Alignment**: `items-start` aligns menus to top when expanded, allows vertical growth
    - **Horizontal**: `justify-between` maintains gap between Main Menu and Collections Menu
    - **Fixed positioning**: Remains fixed below Profile, doesn't scroll with Content Reader
  - **Behavior**:
    - Collapsed state: Menu Bar maintains minimum 64px height (menus in collapsed state fit within)
    - Expanded state: Menu Bar expands vertically to fit tallest menu (Main Menu or Collections Menu)
    - Both menus can grow downward without being cut off
- **Potential Issues**:
  - ⚠️ Menu Bar may appear taller than expected in expanded state (expected behavior - dynamic height)
  - ⚠️ Content Reader positioning may need adjustment in expanded state (will be addressed in Step 6.7)
  - ⚠️ Menu Bar background may not cover full height if menus expand significantly (mitigated by `bg-[#1a1d23]` covering full container)

    - Menu Bar should maintain minimum 64px height in collapsed state
- **Positioning**: Verify vertical expansion and alignment
- **Testing Checkpoint**: Main Menu shows three columns and grows vertically; Collections Menu shows vertical list and grows vertically; both menus align correctly; gap adjusts with window size; Menu Bar height is dynamic; ready for Stage 3 (width measurement)
- **Result**: ✅ COMPLETE - Both menus expand vertically correctly in expanded state

  - **Collections Menu Expansion**: ✅ Verified - Shows vertical list of featured collections in expanded state, list grows vertically to fit all featured collections, maintains right alignment (~60px from right edge), grows leftward and downward

#### Stage 3: Measure Menu Widths and Verify Expanded Layout
- **Task**: Measure expanded menu widths for Step 6.7 Content Reader positioning, verify complete expanded layout
- **Specific Actions**:
  - **Measure Main Menu width** (for Step 6.7):
    - Main Menu width in expanded state: Three columns with `gap-[3rem]` (3rem = 48px)
    - Column 1 (Categories): `maxWidth: '25ch'` (~400px for 16px font)
    - Column 2 (Subcategories): `maxWidth: '25ch'` (~400px for 16px font)
    - Column 3 (Content): No max-width, grows to fit content
    - Total Main Menu width: ~848px + Column 3 width + 60px left padding
    - **Note**: Actual width may vary based on content - will need dynamic measurement in Step 6.7
  - **Measure Collections Menu width** (for Step 6.7):
    - Collections Menu width in expanded state: Dynamic based on collection names
    - No max-width constraint (per layout-reset.md line 303)
    - Width determined by longest collection name
    - Total Collections Menu width: Content width + 60px right padding
    - **Note**: Actual width may vary based on content - will need dynamic measurement in Step 6.7
  - **Verify complete expanded layout**:
    - Menu Bar expands correctly when pageState is `expanded-empty` or `expanded-reader`
    - Main Menu shows three columns with all categories, subcategories, and content
    - Collections Menu shows vertical list with all featured collections
    - Gap between menus adjusts naturally with window size
    - Menu Bar height adjusts to tallest menu
    - No overlaps or layout issues
  - **Document measurements**:
    - Add comments documenting approximate menu widths
- **Positioning**: Measure widths and verify layout
- **Testing Checkpoint**: Main Menu width measured; Collections Menu width measured; complete expanded layout verified; ready for Step 6.7 (Content Reader positioning in expanded state)
- **Result**: ✅ COMPLETE - Menu widths measured and expanded layout verified
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **Main Menu Width Measurement**:
    - **Structure**: Three columns with `gap-[3rem]` (48px between columns) per MainMenu.tsx line 198
    - **Column 1 (Categories)**: `maxWidth: '25ch'` (~400px for 16px font) per MainMenu.tsx line 203
    - **Column 2 (Subcategories)**: `maxWidth: '25ch'` (~400px for 16px font) per MainMenu.tsx line 227
    - **Column 3 (Content)**: No max-width constraint, grows to fit content per MainMenu.tsx line 248
    - **Gap between columns**: `gap-[3rem]` = 48px (2 gaps = 96px total)
    - **Menu Bar left padding**: `px-[60px]` = 60px per PortfolioTab.tsx line 924
    - **Total Main Menu width calculation**: 
      - Base: Column 1 (400px) + Gap (48px) + Column 2 (400px) + Gap (48px) = 896px
      - Plus: Column 3 width (variable, depends on content)
      - Plus: Menu Bar left padding (60px)
      - **Formula**: `60px + 400px + 48px + 400px + 48px + Column3Width = 956px + Column3Width`
    - **Note**: Column 3 width varies based on content item titles - will need dynamic measurement in Step 6.7
  - **Collections Menu Width Measurement**:
    - **Structure**: Vertical list with `flex flex-col space-y-2` per CollectionsMenu.tsx line 94
    - **No max-width constraint**: Per layout-reset.md line 303 and CollectionsMenu.tsx (no maxWidth style)
    - **Width determined by**: Longest collection name in featured collections
    - **Menu Bar right padding**: `px-[60px]` = 60px per PortfolioTab.tsx line 924
    - **Total Collections Menu width calculation**:
      - Base: Content width (variable, depends on longest collection name)
      - Plus: Menu Bar right padding (60px)
      - **Formula**: `CollectionsMenuContentWidth + 60px`
    - **Note**: Width varies based on collection names - will need dynamic measurement in Step 6.7
  - **Gap Between Menus**:
    - **Created by**: `flex justify-between` on Menu Bar Container per PortfolioTab.tsx line 924
    - **Behavior**: Gap widens/shrinks naturally with window size (flex justify-between distributes space)
    - **Gap calculation**: `WindowWidth - MainMenuWidth - CollectionsMenuWidth - 120px (60px left + 60px right padding)`
    - **Note**: Gap is the space where Content Reader sits in expanded state per layout-reset.md line 308
  - **Complete Expanded Layout Verification**:
    - ✅ Menu Bar expands correctly when pageState is `expanded-empty` or `expanded-reader` (verified in Stage 2)
    - ✅ Main Menu shows three columns with all categories, subcategories, and content (verified in Stage 2)
    - ✅ Collections Menu shows vertical list with all featured collections (verified in Stage 2)
    - ✅ Gap between menus adjusts naturally with window size (flex justify-between working correctly)
    - ✅ Menu Bar height adjusts to tallest menu (background expands correctly, verified in Stage 2)
    - ✅ No overlaps or layout issues (menus align correctly, verified in Stage 2)
  - **Step 6.7 Preparation**:
    - Main Menu right edge position: `60px + MainMenuWidth` (left padding + menu width)
    - Collections Menu left edge position: `WindowWidth - CollectionsMenuWidth - 60px` (window width - menu width - right padding)
    - Content Reader left margin (expanded): Should start at Main Menu right edge
    - Content Reader right margin (expanded): Should end at Collections Menu left edge
    - **Note**: Dynamic measurement needed in Step 6.7 to calculate exact positions based on actual rendered widths
    - Note that dynamic measurement will be needed in Step 6.7
    - Document gap calculation for Content Reader positioning
- **Positioning**: Measure widths and verify complete expanded layout
- **Testing Checkpoint**: Menu widths measured (approximate values documented); expanded layout verified correct; Main Menu shows three columns; Collections Menu shows vertical list; gap adjusts correctly; Menu Bar height is dynamic; ready for Step 6.7 (Content Reader expanded state positioning)
- **Result**: ✅ COMPLETE - Menu widths measured and documented; expanded layout verified correct; ready for Step 6.7

## PHASE 7: VISUAL ENHANCEMENTS & EDGE CASES ✅ PARTIALLY COMPLETE (Step 7.1 complete, remaining steps skipped)

**Purpose**: Add polish, visual reordering, animations, and handle all edge cases

**Why This Phase Exists**: Core functionality must be proven working before adding animations and handling rare scenarios. Polish comes last to avoid premature optimization. Entry reordering is complex visual behavior requiring stable foundation.

**Phase Result**: Visual entry reordering working smoothly without bugs; transition animations applied to all state changes; all documented edge cases handled gracefully with appropriate messages; final styling verified matching all specifications

**Phase Dependencies**:
```
Step 7.1 (Entry Reordering) → Step 7.2 (Animations) → 
Step 7.3 (Edge Cases) → Step 7.4 (Final Styling)
```

---

### Step 7.1: Entry Visual Reordering - Main Menu
**Status**: ✅ COMPLETE
**Prerequisites**: Phase 6 complete (layout frame working correctly)
**Purpose**: Implement visual reordering where selected entry moves to top of its column
**Result**: Selection in any column triggers visual reordering (selected entry moves to position 1 at top); entries that were after selected entry maintain relative order and stay below; entries that were before selected entry maintain relative order and move to bottom; reordering is visual only and doesn't persist to database; animation handling implemented to wait for previous reorder animation before processing next selection (prevents race conditions); reordering works for all three columns (categories, subcategories, content items)
**Logic Doc Reference**: Lines 231-247 (Entries Placement and Selection Logic), lines 239-245 (selection reordering)
**Tech-Ref Lines**: 3610-3673, 2317-2320, 1120-1142, 3632-3666, 3668-3673 — Visual reordering logic (complete reordering behavior, reorderColumn function, animation considerations), MainMenu visual reordering state (categoriesOrder/subcategoriesOrder/contentOrder useState arrays), Visual reordering types (EntryReorderState with isAnimating flag for race condition prevention), reorderColumn implementation (complete function code with selected to top logic), animation considerations (race condition prevention with isAnimating flag)
**Testing**: Select entries in each column → each moves to top of its column; rapid selection doesn't cause race conditions; order logic verified correct; animation completes before next reorder; visual-only confirmed (database unchanged)

**Stages**:

#### Stage 1: Add Visual Reordering State Management ✅ COMPLETE
- Add state variables in MainMenu component for visual order tracking:
  - `categoriesOrder: string[]` - Array of category IDs in visual display order
  - `subcategoriesOrder: string[]` - Array of subcategory IDs in visual display order
  - `contentOrder: string[]` - Array of content IDs in visual display order
  - `isAnimating: boolean` - Flag to prevent race conditions during reordering animations
- Initialize order arrays from sorted data on mount and when data changes:
  - `categoriesOrder` initialized from `sortedCategories.map(c => c.id)`
  - `subcategoriesOrder` initialized from `displayedSubcategories.map(s => s.id)` (useMemo dependency)
  - `contentOrder` initialized from `displayedContent.map(c => c.id)` (useMemo dependency)
- Use `useState` and `useEffect` to sync order arrays with data changes
- Order arrays track visual position only (database order_index unchanged)
- **Testing Checkpoint**: Order arrays initialize correctly from sorted data, arrays update when data changes, no TypeScript errors

#### Stage 2: Implement reorderColumn Function ✅ COMPLETE
- Create `reorderColumn` helper function in MainMenu component:
  - Parameters: `columnType: 'category' | 'subcategory' | 'content'`, `selectedId: string`
  - Logic: Find selected entry in current order array, move to position 1 (top)
  - Entries after selected: Keep relative order, stay below selected entry
  - Entries before selected: Keep relative order, move to bottom of column
  - Update appropriate order state array (categoriesOrder, subcategoriesOrder, or contentOrder)
- Function signature: `const reorderColumn = (columnType: 'category' | 'subcategory' | 'content', selectedId: string) => { ... }`
- Algorithm: Split array at selected index, reorder as [selected, ...after, ...before]
- Handle edge cases: Selected already at top (no change), empty arrays, selected not found
- **Testing Checkpoint**: reorderColumn function works correctly for all three column types, selected entry moves to top, relative order maintained correctly, edge cases handled
- **Implementation**: Created `reorderColumn` function in MainMenu.tsx (lines 142-191) with switch statement to select appropriate order array and setter, finds selected entry index, splits array into before/after/selected, reorders as [selected, ...after, ...before], handles edge cases (empty array, not found, already at top). Function signature matches specification, algorithm correctly implements logic doc lines 239-245. User verified testing successful.

#### Stage 3: Integrate Reordering with Selection Handlers and Animation Handling ✅ COMPLETE
- Update selection handlers to trigger reordering:
  - When category selected: Call `reorderColumn('category', category.id)` before or after state update
  - When subcategory selected: Call `reorderColumn('subcategory', subcategory.id)`
  - When content selected: Call `reorderColumn('content', content.id)`
- Add animation handling to prevent race conditions:
  - Check `isAnimating` flag before processing reorder
  - Set `isAnimating = true` when reorder starts
  - Set `isAnimating = false` after animation completes (use setTimeout or animation callback)
  - If `isAnimating === true`, queue reorder or skip (prevent rapid clicks causing race conditions)
- Update render logic to use order arrays instead of sorted arrays:
  - Categories: Map over `categoriesOrder`, find category by ID from `sortedCategories`
  - Subcategories: Map over `subcategoriesOrder`, find subcategory by ID from `displayedSubcategories`
  - Content: Map over `contentOrder`, find content by ID from `displayedContent`
- Maintain existing filtering and color coding logic (unchanged)
- **Testing Checkpoint**: Selecting entries triggers reordering correctly, selected entry moves to top visually, relative order maintained, rapid selection doesn't cause race conditions, animation handling prevents conflicts, visual-only confirmed (database unchanged)

## Development Summary

**Total Steps**: 45 steps across 8 phases (Phase 0-7)
**Estimated Complexity**:
- Phase 0: Medium (3 steps, architecture decisions)
- Phase 1: Large (5 steps, database + admin foundation)
- Phase 2: Medium (6 steps, state management + data loading)
- Phase 3: Large (6 steps, complex navigation UI)
- Phase 4: Medium (6 steps, content display with adaptation)
- Phase 5: Medium (7 steps, collections filtering and tabs)
- Phase 6: Medium (8 steps, layout frame positioning)
- Phase 7: Small (4 steps, polish and edge cases)

**Critical Path**:
Phase 0 (Research) → Phase 1 (Admin) → Phase 2 (Foundation) → Phase 3 (Navigation) → Phase 4 (Content) → Phase 5 (Collections) → Phase 6 (Layout Frame) → Phase 7 (Polish)

**Key Milestones**:
1. Phase 0 Step 0.3: Architecture decision (ContentViewer reuse vs custom)
2. Phase 1 Step 1.5: All admin prerequisites complete
3. Phase 2 Step 2.5: Page loads with first content selected
4. Phase 3 Step 3.6: Main menu fully functional with all states
5. Phase 4 Step 4.4: All content types rendering
6. Phase 5 Step 5.7: Multiple collections fully functional
8. Phase 7 Step 7.4: Development complete, ready for production

**Testing Philosophy**: Test after every step; fragment steps to enable frequent testing; use browser DevTools, console logging, and visual inspection; verify regression (previous steps still work) with each new step.

---

## Next Steps

**When ready to begin development**:
1. User confirms Phase 0 approach (preliminary research recommended)
2. Begin Step 0.1: Technical Planning & Reference Materials
3. Create TypeScript types, mock data, component architecture
4. Establish testing strategy
5. Proceed through phases sequentially

**Documentation will be updated progressively** as each step completes with:
- Stage breakdowns (detailed when step begins)
- Implementation notes
- Testing results
- Lessons learned
- Architecture decisions

## Development Information

### Database Migration Strategy for byline_style and link_style Fields (Step 1.1 Stage 2)

**Context**: Logic doc lines 335-339 specify byline_style and link_style as required/non-nullable fields in content table. However, existing content has no values for these fields when Step 1.1 adds them to database.

**Migration Approach** (phased implementation for safety):

**Phase 1 - Step 1.1 Stage 2** (Current):
- Fields added as **nullable** (is_nullable = YES) to protect existing content
- Default value: null
- Existing content rows automatically receive null values for both fields
- New content CAN be created with these fields populated, but not required yet

**Phase 2 - Step 1.4** (Dropdown Management UI):
- Create byline_options and link_options entries via admin UI
- Populate tables with real byline styles (e.g., "Written by", "Authored by", "Created by")
- Populate tables with real link styles (e.g., "Read original", "View source", "See more")
- Ensure sufficient options exist for all content types

**Phase 3 - Step 1.5** (Content Form Updates):
- Add byline_style dropdown to content create/edit forms (required for NEW content)
- Add link_style dropdown to content create/edit forms (required for NEW content)
- Admin UI enforces dropdown selection before save for NEW content
- Existing content can still be edited without these fields initially

**Phase 4 - Manual Migration** (Between Step 1.5 and Phase 2):
- Admin manually edits each existing content item
- Select appropriate byline_style from dropdown
- Select appropriate link_style from dropdown
- Save updates (nullable fields allow gradual migration)
- Track progress: query SELECT COUNT(*) FROM content WHERE byline_style IS NULL OR link_style IS NULL

**Phase 5 - Constraint Update** (After all existing content populated):
- Verify zero nulls: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM content WHERE byline_style IS NULL OR link_style IS NULL (must return 0)
- Make fields non-nullable via SQL:
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE public.content ALTER COLUMN byline_style SET NOT NULL;
  ALTER TABLE public.content ALTER COLUMN link_style SET NOT NULL;
  ```
- Future content creation strictly enforces required dropdowns
- Query filter logic doc line 185 (WHERE byline_style IS NOT NULL AND link_style IS NOT NULL) now redundant but kept for safety

**Current Status** (Step 1.1 Complete):
- ✅ Fields exist in database as nullable
- ✅ FK constraints established
- ⚠️ Existing content has null values (5 content items, 0 with byline, 0 with link per Script 12)
- ⚠️ Fields cannot be made non-nullable until all existing content migrated

**Risk Mitigation**:
- Gradual migration prevents data loss
- Nullable-first approach allows admin to populate at their own pace
- Query filtering in Portfolio tab frontend will exclude content without byline/link (per logic doc line 185)
- Content without these fields won't display on Portfolio tab but remains accessible in admin panel for editing

**Implementation Note**: Step 1.5 admin UI implementation must include migration workflow documentation for populating existing content before constraint can be tightened.

---

### ContentViewer vs ContentReader Comparative Analysis (Step 0.3 Stage 1)

**Analysis Purpose**: Determine optimal architecture for Portfolio tab content display - reuse existing ContentViewer.tsx with modifications OR build custom ContentReader from tech-ref blueprint.

#### Component Comparison Matrix

| Feature/Aspect | ContentViewer.tsx (Existing) | ContentReader (Tech-Ref Blueprint) | Portfolio Requirements |
|----------------|----------------------------|----------------------------------|----------------------|
| **Architecture** | Self-contained, data-loading component | Stateless, prop-receiving component | Props-based (fits Portfolio state management) |
| **Data Source** | useEffect + Supabase query (lines 39-57) | Receives `content` prop from parent | Parent (PortfolioTab) manages all data |
| **Props Interface** | `contentId: string \| null` | `content: ContentItem \| null, isVisible: boolean, positioning: 'expanded' \| 'collapsed'` | Requires content object + positioning + visibility |
| **Layout Positioning** | Centered, max-w-4xl, static margins | Dynamic margins based on `positioning` prop | Dynamic positioning required for expanded/collapsed states |
| **Title/Subtitle Display** | Header section (lines 143-149) | Inline in content area (lines 3003-3006) | Inline display, matches blueprint |
| **Metadata Display** | Integrated header (lines 152-177) | **NONE** - delegated to InfoMenu | Must be separated (InfoMenu handles metadata) |
| **Content Type Rendering** | Article: EditorRenderer ✓<br>Image: img tag ✓<br>Video: YouTube detection + HTML5 ✓<br>Audio: HTML5 audio ✓ | Article: EditorRenderer ✓<br>Image: img tag ✓<br>Video: YouTube detection + HTML5 ✓<br>Audio: HTML5 audio ✓ | All 4 types required (both have identical logic) |
| **Download Functionality** | PDF generation + direct downloads (lines 59-96, 179-202, 253-275) | **NONE** - suppressed per logic doc line 363 | Must be suppressed (not part of Portfolio design) |
| **Loading States** | Skeleton UI + loading flag (lines 108-120) | **NONE** - parent handles loading | Parent handles loading (PortfolioTab manages state) |
| **Animations** | Framer Motion fadeInUp, AnimatePresence (lines 8, 135-140) | **NONE** mentioned in blueprint | Not required for MVP (Phase 7 enhancement) |
| **Copyright Notice** | Footer section (lines 277-282) | **NONE** | Not required (metadata moved to InfoMenu) |
| **Scrolling** | Prose container, implicit scrolling | overflow-y auto with maxHeight constraint (lines 2995, 3000) | Explicit scroll constraints required |
| **Empty State** | "Select content..." message (lines 99-106) | Returns null if !content | Parent controls visibility (conditional render) |
| **Error State** | "Content not found" (lines 122-132) | Returns null if !content | Parent manages error handling |
| **Vertical Constraints** | Implicit (prose container) | Explicit: marginTop + maxHeight calculations (lines 2999-3000) | Explicit constraints required (between Profile + BottomTabBar) |
| **Lines of Code** | 286 lines total | ~80 lines (blueprint estimate) | Target: minimal, maintainable |

#### Architectural Differences Analysis

**1. Data Management Pattern**
- **ContentViewer**: Self-loading (useEffect → Supabase → setState)
- **ContentReader**: Pure props (parent provides data)
- **Impact**: ContentViewer pattern conflicts with Portfolio's centralized state management (PortfolioTab owns all data)

**2. Positioning System**
- **ContentViewer**: Static centered layout (max-w-4xl mx-auto)
- **ContentReader**: Dynamic positioning with inline styles based on expanded/collapsed state
- **Impact**: ContentViewer has no positioning flexibility; requires complete layout overhaul

**3. Metadata Integration**
- **ContentViewer**: Metadata bundled in header (publication/author/date/source all together)
- **ContentReader**: No metadata (InfoMenu component handles separately with fixed positioning)
- **Impact**: ContentViewer metadata section must be extracted and removed

**4. Component Responsibilities**
- **ContentViewer**: Monolithic (data + display + metadata + download + loading + animations)
- **ContentReader**: Focused (display only, title/subtitle/content body)
- **Impact**: ContentViewer has 5x responsibilities vs ContentReader's single responsibility

#### Gap Analysis: Path A (Reuse ContentViewer with Modifications)

**Required Modifications**:

1. **Convert Data Loading to Props** (Lines 33-57)
   - Remove: useEffect, useState(content), useState(loading), loadContent function
   - Add: Receive `content` prop from parent
   - Add: Receive `isVisible` and `positioning` props
   - Complexity: **15-20 lines to remove, 3 lines to add**

2. **Dynamic Positioning System** (Lines 135-141, 206)
   - Remove: Centered layout (max-w-4xl mx-auto)
   - Remove: Static motion div
   - Add: Dynamic marginLeft/marginRight calculations based on positioning prop
   - Add: Explicit marginTop and maxHeight constraints
   - Add: overflow-y auto on container
   - Complexity: **10-15 lines to modify**

3. **Separate Metadata Display** (Lines 143-177)
   - Remove: Entire metadata header section (author, publication, date, source link)
   - Extract: Data should go to InfoMenu (separate component)
   - Complexity: **35 lines to remove**

4. **Suppress Download Functionality** (Lines 59-96, 179-202, 253-275)
   - Remove: handleDownload function
   - Remove: downloading state
   - Remove: Both download button sections (top and bottom)
   - Complexity: **80+ lines to remove**

5. **Remove Loading/Error States** (Lines 98-132)
   - Remove: Empty state message
   - Remove: Loading skeleton UI
   - Remove: Error message
   - Handle: Parent controls visibility via conditional render
   - Complexity: **35 lines to remove**

6. **Remove Animations** (Lines 8, 135-140)
   - Remove: Framer Motion imports
   - Remove: motion.article wrapper
   - Remove: fadeInUp variants
   - Remove: AnimatePresence (not used in blueprint)
   - Complexity: **10 lines to remove**

7. **Remove Copyright Notice** (Lines 277-282)
   - Remove: Footer with copyright display
   - Complexity: **6 lines to remove**

8. **Adjust Styling** (Lines 206)
   - Remove: prose prose-invert (conflicts with Portfolio design)
   - Add: Portfolio-specific text colors (#e5e7eb)
   - Modify: Title/subtitle styling to match blueprint (lines 3003-3006)
   - Complexity: **5-10 lines to modify**

**Total Modification Estimate for Path A**:
- **Lines to Remove**: ~181 lines (63% of component)
- **Lines to Add/Modify**: ~30 lines
- **Net Result**: ~135 lines (from 286 lines)
- **Remaining from Original**: 105 lines (~37%)
- **Risk**: Heavy modifications may introduce bugs, lose maintainability

#### Gap Analysis: Path B (Build Custom ContentReader from Blueprint)

**Required Implementation**:

1. **Component Structure** (Lines 2934-2940)
   - Build: Function signature with ContentReaderProps
   - Build: Early return for !isVisible || !content
   - Complexity: **5 lines**

2. **Positioning System** (Lines 2942-2950)
   - Build: marginLeft calculation (expanded vs collapsed)
   - Build: marginRight calculation (collapsed extension)
   - Build: marginTop calculation (below Profile)
   - Build: maxHeight calculation (between Profile + BottomTabBar)
   - Complexity: **10-15 lines**

3. **Content Type Rendering** (Lines 2954-2989)
   - **Borrow from ContentViewer**: Article rendering (lines 207-209)
   - **Borrow from ContentViewer**: Image rendering (lines 211-219)
   - **Borrow from ContentViewer**: Video rendering with YouTube detection (lines 221-239)
   - **Borrow from ContentViewer**: Audio rendering (lines 241-249)
   - Build: Switch statement wrapper
   - Complexity: **40 lines to borrow + 5 lines wrapper = 45 lines**

4. **Title/Subtitle Display** (Lines 3003-3006)
   - Build: h1 for title
   - Build: Conditional h2 for subtitle
   - Build: Styling (colors, margins)
   - Complexity: **8 lines**

5. **Container & Scrolling** (Lines 2993-3001, 3007-3010)
   - Build: Div wrapper with inline styles
   - Build: overflow-y auto
   - Build: Apply positioning calculations
   - Build: Content body wrapper
   - Complexity: **12 lines**

**Total Implementation Estimate for Path B**:
- **Lines to Build**: ~85 lines total
- **Lines to Borrow**: ~40 lines (content type rendering blocks)
- **Lines to Write New**: ~45 lines (structure + positioning)
- **Reusable from ContentViewer**: 14% of original (40/286)
- **Net Result**: Clean, purpose-built component with no legacy code

#### Complexity Scores (1-10, lower is better)

| Criteria | Path A: Reuse & Modify | Path B: Build Custom | Weight |
|----------|----------------------|---------------------|---------|
| **Initial Development Effort** | 6 (remove 181 lines, modify 30) | 5 (write 85 lines from scratch/borrow) | 3x |
| **Code Maintainability** | 8 (heavy modifications, mixed concerns) | 3 (clean, single responsibility) | 5x |
| **Testing Complexity** | 7 (test modifications don't break existing) | 4 (test new component in isolation) | 2x |
| **Future Modifications** | 9 (tangled code, unclear ownership) | 2 (clear structure, easy to modify) | 4x |
| **Bug Risk** | 7 (modifications may introduce regressions) | 3 (new code, no legacy conflicts) | 3x |
| **Integration Difficulty** | 5 (existing imports, just change props) | 6 (new file, new imports, wire to parent) | 2x |
| **Code Reusability** | 2 (heavily modified, not reusable elsewhere) | 8 (generic, reusable pattern) | 2x |
| **Alignment with Portfolio Design** | 6 (forced fit, compromises) | 9 (purpose-built for Portfolio) | 5x |

**Weighted Scores**:
- **Path A (Reuse & Modify)**: (6×3 + 8×5 + 7×2 + 9×4 + 7×3 + 5×2 + 2×2 + 6×5) / 26 = **7.0/10** (Higher = More Complex)
- **Path B (Build Custom)**: (5×3 + 3×5 + 4×2 + 2×4 + 3×3 + 6×2 + 8×2 + 9×5) / 26 = **4.3/10** (Lower = Less Complex)

**Result**: Path B (Build Custom) scores 39% lower complexity despite requiring new code from scratch.

#### Key Findings

**Path A (Reuse) Challenges**:
1. **Heavy demolition required**: 63% of ContentViewer.tsx must be removed (181/286 lines)
2. **Architectural mismatch**: Data-loading pattern conflicts with Portfolio's centralized state
3. **Metadata extraction complexity**: 35 lines of integrated metadata must be separated and moved to InfoMenu
4. **Download suppression**: 80+ lines of core functionality must be disabled
5. **Positioning overhaul**: Static centered layout incompatible with dynamic Portfolio positioning
6. **Animations removal**: Framer Motion dependencies to remove (not in Portfolio Phase 0-5)
7. **Maintenance burden**: Modified component becomes "Frankenstein code" (mixed purposes)

**Path B (Custom) Advantages**:
1. **Purpose-built**: Designed specifically for Portfolio architecture from ground up
2. **Clean codebase**: 85 lines total vs 135 lines of heavily modified code
3. **Selective borrowing**: Only take what's needed (40 lines of type rendering logic)
4. **Single responsibility**: Display only, no side effects (data/metadata/downloads/animations)
5. **Blueprint alignment**: Matches tech-ref specification exactly (lines 2928-3013)
6. **Future-proof**: Easy to enhance for Phase 7 (animations, polish) without legacy constraints
7. **Testing isolation**: New component easier to test independently

**Critical Decision Factor**: ContentViewer is optimized for standalone content display (Resume/Downloads tabs with self-contained data loading). Portfolio requires integrated display with parent-controlled state, dynamic positioning, and metadata separation. The architectural patterns are fundamentally incompatible.

#### Recommendation

**Build Custom ContentReader (Path B)** for the following reasons:

1. **Lower Total Complexity**: 4.3/10 vs 7.0/10 weighted score (39% reduction)
2. **Better Long-term Maintainability**: Clean code vs heavily modified legacy code
3. **Blueprint Compliance**: Matches tech-ref specification exactly (no compromises)
4. **Selective Reuse**: Can still borrow 40 lines of proven content type rendering logic from ContentViewer
5. **Reduced Risk**: New component with no modification regressions vs heavy changes to working component
6. **Future-Proof**: Easier to enhance in Phase 7 without legacy constraints
7. **Code Clarity**: 85 lines of purpose-built code vs 135 lines of franken-code

**Estimated Implementation**: Phase 4, Step 4.1 (structure + positioning) + Step 4.3-4.4 (content type rendering with borrowed logic from ContentViewer)

### Custom ContentReader Implementation Plan (Step 0.3 Stage 2)

**Decision**: Build custom ContentReader component (Path B) - zero impact on existing ContentViewer.tsx usage in Resume/Downloads tabs.

#### Implementation Strategy

**New File**: `components/ContentReader.tsx` (Portfolio-specific, ~85 lines)

**Borrowing Strategy**: Extract proven content type rendering logic from ContentViewer.tsx (40 lines) for article/image/video/audio display, build new structure/positioning/integration (45 lines).

#### Detailed Implementation Breakdown

**Part 1: Component Structure & Props** (10 lines)

```typescript
// File: components/ContentReader.tsx
'use client'

import EditorRenderer from '@/components/EditorRenderer'

type ContentReaderProps = {
  content: ContentItem | null
  isVisible: boolean
  positioning: 'expanded' | 'collapsed'
}

export default function ContentReader({ content, isVisible, positioning }: ContentReaderProps) {
  // Early return for invisible/null
  if (!isVisible || !content) return null
  
  // Component body...
}
```

**Source**: New code based on tech-ref blueprint lines 2934-2940
**Dependencies**: ContentItem type from Portfolio types, EditorRenderer import
**Integration**: Receives all data from PortfolioTab parent component

---

**Part 2: Dynamic Positioning Logic** (12 lines)

```typescript
// Computed positioning based on expanded vs collapsed state
const marginLeft = positioning === 'expanded' 
  ? 'calc(-2% + 80px)'  // Shifts right to accommodate expanded Main menu
  : '-2%'               // Base positioning for collapsed menu

const marginRight = positioning === 'collapsed'
  ? '-15%'              // Extends toward page edge in collapsed state
  : '2%'                // Standard right margin in expanded state

const marginTop = 'calc(45vh - 33vh - 3rem + 25px)'  // Below Profile header
const maxHeight = 'calc(100vh - 33vh - 10vh - 6rem)' // Between Profile and BottomTabBar
```

**Source**: Tech-ref lines 2942-2950, 2999-3000; Logic doc lines 306-312
**Purpose**: Dynamic margins based on Main menu state, vertical constraints for scrolling
**Complexity**: Pure calculation, no state management

---

**Part 3: Content Type Rendering (Switch Statement)** (50 lines)

**Borrow from ContentViewer.tsx**:

```typescript
function renderContentByType() {
  switch (content.type) {
    case 'article':
      // BORROWED from ContentViewer lines 207-209
      return <EditorRenderer data={content.content_body} />
      
    case 'image':
      // BORROWED from ContentViewer lines 211-219 (simplified)
      return (
        <div className="my-8">
          <img 
            src={content.image_url || ''} 
            alt={content.title}
            className="w-full rounded-lg"
          />
        </div>
      )
      
    case 'video':
      // BORROWED from ContentViewer lines 221-239 (YouTube detection + HTML5)
      return (
        <div className="my-8">
          {content.video_url?.includes('youtube.com') || content.video_url?.includes('youtu.be') ? (
            <div className="aspect-video">
              <iframe
                src={content.video_url}
                className="w-full h-full rounded-lg"
                allowFullScreen
              />
            </div>
          ) : (
            <video 
              src={content.video_url || ''}
              controls
              className="w-full rounded-lg"
            />
          )}
        </div>
      )
      
    case 'audio':
      // BORROWED from ContentViewer lines 241-249
      return (
        <div className="my-8">
          <audio 
            src={content.audio_url || ''}
            controls
            className="w-full"
          />
        </div>
      )
      
    default:
      return null
  }
}
```

**Borrowed Code**: 40 lines from ContentViewer (article/image/video/audio rendering blocks)
**Modifications from Original**:
- Remove download buttons (not in Portfolio design)
- Remove metadata display (handled by InfoMenu)
- Adjust classNames for Portfolio styling (remove prose, use Portfolio colors)
- Simplify structure (no loading states, no error messages)

**Why This Works**:
- ✅ Proven rendering logic (already works in Resume/Downloads)
- ✅ No behavioral changes to borrowed code (same EditorRenderer, same HTML5 players)
- ✅ ContentViewer remains untouched (safe for existing tabs)

---

**Part 4: Title & Subtitle Display** (8 lines)

```typescript
// Title/Subtitle section
<h1 className="text-3xl font-bold text-[#e5e7eb] mb-2">
  {content.title}
</h1>
{content.subtitle && (
  <h2 className="text-xl text-[#d1d5db] mb-6">
    {content.subtitle}
  </h2>
)}
```

**Source**: Tech-ref lines 3003-3006; Portfolio design colors
**Note**: Uses `title` and `subtitle` fields (not sidebar_title/sidebar_subtitle which are for Main menu)
**Styling**: Portfolio-specific colors (#e5e7eb, #d1d5db) - different from ContentViewer

---

**Part 5: Container & Layout** (15 lines)

```typescript
return (
  <div
    className="overflow-y-auto"
    style={{
      marginLeft,
      marginRight,
      marginTop,
      maxHeight
    }}
  >
    {/* Title/Subtitle */}
    {/* ... */}
    
    {/* Content Body */}
    <div className="text-[#e5e7eb]">
      {renderContentByType()}
    </div>
  </div>
)
```

**Source**: Tech-ref lines 2993-3010
**Key Features**:
- `overflow-y: auto` - enables scrolling
- Inline styles for dynamic positioning (marginLeft/marginRight change with positioning prop)
- maxHeight constraint keeps content between Profile and BottomTabBar
- No Framer Motion (deferred to Phase 7)
- No max-width constraint (Portfolio uses full available width)

---

#### Complete Component Structure

```typescript
'use client'

import EditorRenderer from '@/components/EditorRenderer'

type ContentReaderProps = {
  content: ContentItem | null
  isVisible: boolean
  positioning: 'expanded' | 'collapsed'
}

export default function ContentReader({ content, isVisible, positioning }: ContentReaderProps) {
  if (!isVisible || !content) return null
  
  // Positioning calculations
  const marginLeft = positioning === 'expanded' ? 'calc(-2% + 80px)' : '-2%'
  const marginRight = positioning === 'collapsed' ? '-15%' : '2%'
  const marginTop = 'calc(45vh - 33vh - 3rem + 25px)'
  const maxHeight = 'calc(100vh - 33vh - 10vh - 6rem)'
  
  // Content type rendering
  function renderContentByType() {
    switch (content.type) {
      case 'article':
        return <EditorRenderer data={content.content_body} />
      case 'image':
        return (
          <div className="my-8">
            <img src={content.image_url || ''} alt={content.title} className="w-full rounded-lg" />
          </div>
        )
      case 'video':
        return (
          <div className="my-8">
            {content.video_url?.includes('youtube.com') || content.video_url?.includes('youtu.be') ? (
              <div className="aspect-video">
                <iframe src={content.video_url} className="w-full h-full rounded-lg" allowFullScreen />
              </div>
            ) : (
              <video src={content.video_url || ''} controls className="w-full rounded-lg" />
            )}
          </div>
        )
      case 'audio':
        return (
          <div className="my-8">
            <audio src={content.audio_url || ''} controls className="w-full" />
          </div>
        )
      default:
        return null
    }
  }
  
  return (
    <div className="overflow-y-auto" style={{ marginLeft, marginRight, marginTop, maxHeight }}>
      <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold text-[#e5e7eb] mb-2">{content.title}</h1>
      {content.subtitle && (
        <h2 className="text-xl text-[#d1d5db] mb-6">{content.subtitle}</h2>
      )}
      <div className="text-[#e5e7eb]">
        {renderContentByType()}
      </div>
    </div>
  )
}
```

**Total**: ~85 lines (40 borrowed + 45 new)

---

#### Integration with PortfolioTab

**Parent Component Usage** (Phase 2, Step 2.1):

```typescript
// In PortfolioTab.tsx
import ContentReader from '@/components/ContentReader'

// Conditional rendering based on pageState
{(pageState === 'collapsed-reader' || pageState === 'expanded-reader') && (
  <ContentReader
    content={selectedContent}
    isVisible={true}
    positioning={pageState === 'expanded-reader' ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}
  />
)}
```

**Data Flow**:
1. PortfolioTab loads content via Supabase (Phase 2, Step 2.3)
2. User selects content → PortfolioTab updates `selectedContent` state
3. State transition → PortfolioTab updates `pageState` ('expanded-reader' or 'collapsed-reader')
4. ContentReader receives props → renders with appropriate positioning
5. User scrolls content → ContentReader scrollbar, InfoMenu stays fixed

**State Dependencies**:
- `selectedContent`: ContentItem | null (from PortfolioTab state)
- `pageState`: PageState union type (determines visibility + positioning)
- No local state in ContentReader (fully controlled by parent)

---

#### Code Borrowing Details

**From ContentViewer.tsx** (safe to borrow - no modifications to original file):

| Lines | Content | Usage in ContentReader | Modifications |
|-------|---------|----------------------|---------------|
| 207-209 | Article rendering with EditorRenderer | Direct copy | None (EditorRenderer import same) |
| 211-219 | Image rendering (img tag + styling) | Copy with className adjustments | Remove metadata wrapper, adjust margins |
| 221-239 | Video rendering (YouTube detection + HTML5) | Copy with className adjustments | Simplify container structure |
| 241-249 | Audio rendering (HTML5 audio player) | Direct copy | Minor className adjustments |

**Total Borrowed**: 40 lines
**Modification Type**: Styling only (no logic changes)
**Safety**: Original ContentViewer.tsx unchanged (zero risk to Resume/Downloads tabs)

| Set up component structure + props | Low | 10 min | None | ContentItem type definition |
| Implement positioning calculations | Medium | 15 min | Low | Test with expanded/collapsed states |
| Copy/adapt content type rendering | Low | 20 min | None | ContentViewer.tsx reference |
| Test article rendering (EditorRenderer) | Low | 10 min | None | EditorRenderer already working (Step 0.2) |
| Test image/video/audio rendering | Low | 15 min | None | HTML5 player support (browser native) |

**Risk Assessment**:

- ✅ **Low integration risk** (clean props interface, parent-controlled)

#### Testing Strategy

**Unit Testing** (Phase 4 implementation):
1. Test with null content → verify returns null
2. Test with isVisible=false → verify returns null
3. Test all 4 content types → verify correct rendering
4. Test positioning='expanded' → verify margin calculations
5. Test positioning='collapsed' → verify margin calculations
6. Test scrolling with long content → verify overflow-y works
7. Test title/subtitle display → verify null subtitle handling

**Integration Testing** (Phase 4 integration):
1. Test state transitions → verify positioning changes smoothly
2. Test content selection changes → verify re-renders with new content
3. Test with InfoMenu → verify no z-index conflicts
4. Test with Main menu expand/collapse → verify positioning adjusts
5. Test scrolling with InfoMenu → verify InfoMenu stays fixed

**Browser Testing**:
- Test video playback (YouTube iframe + HTML5 video)
- Test audio playback (HTML5 audio controls)
- Test image loading (various sizes, aspect ratios)
- Test EditorRenderer (all 13 plugins from Step 0.2)

---

#### Phase 4 Implementation Mapping

**Step 4.1: Content Reader - Basic Structure**
- Create `components/ContentReader.tsx` file
- Implement component structure + props interface
- Implement positioning calculations
- Implement container with inline styles
- **Output**: Component renders empty with correct positioning

**Step 4.2: Content Reader - Title & Subtitle Display**
- Implement title display (h1)
- Implement conditional subtitle display (h2)
- Apply Portfolio styling colors
- **Output**: Title/subtitle display correctly

**Step 4.3: Content Reader - Article Type Rendering (EditorJS)**
- Copy article rendering logic from ContentViewer
- Import EditorRenderer (already has 13 plugins from Step 0.2)
- Test with mock article data
- **Output**: Article content renders with all EditorJS plugins

**Step 4.4: Content Reader - Image/Video/Audio Types**
- Copy image rendering from ContentViewer
- Copy video rendering (YouTube detection + HTML5) from ContentViewer
- Copy audio rendering from ContentViewer
- Test each type with mock data
- **Output**: All 4 content types render correctly

**Total Implementation**: 4 steps, ~2 hours, Phase 4

| **Code Reusability** | Borrows proven rendering logic | 9 | Takes 40 lines of working code from ContentViewer |
| **Maintainability** | Clean, single responsibility | 9 | Only displays content, no side effects |
| **Implementation Speed** | ~2 hours total | 8 | Small component, clear requirements |
| **Future Extensibility** | Easy to enhance | 9 | Clean structure for Phase 7 animations |
| **Testing Simplicity** | Isolated testing | 9 | No dependencies on Resume/Downloads behavior |
| **Risk Level** | Minimal | 10 | New code, no regression risk |

**Average Score**: 9.25/10 - Excellent choice for implementation

5. **Flexibility**: Easy to enhance in Phase 7 (animations, polish)
