Step 4.1 Bug Investigation (Right Border & Scrolling Under Main Menu) – User reported two bugs after Stage 4 completion, requested add Stage 5 and Stage 6 to Step 4.1, investigate bugs, update documentation, stand by – **Bug #1 Investigation - Right Border Horizontal Scrolling**: User reported ContentReader right border extends too far right causing horizontal page scrolling. Investigation completed: Current implementation uses `marginRight: '-15%'` for collapsed state (ContentReader.tsx line 64), matches logic doc line 310 specification `margin-right: -15%` ("extends toward page edge, sidebars minimal"), matches mockup.html line 355 specification `margin-right: -15%` ("Negative margin extends to ~5% from page edge"). Root cause identified: Combination of `flex: 1` className (takes available space in flex container) + negative margin `-15%` causes ContentReader to extend beyond viewport width. Issue: Negative margin calculated relative to flex container width (max-w-[1280px] centered), and with `flex: 1` taking available space, the negative margin pushes content beyond viewport boundaries causing horizontal scrolling. Expected behavior per mockup: Content should extend to approximately 5% from right page edge, not beyond viewport. Solution approach: Need to add width constraint (max-width) or adjust margin calculation to prevent overflow while maintaining ~5% from page edge. Expanded state uses `marginRight: '2%'` (line 65) which should not have same issue. **Bug #2 Investigation - Content Visible Under Main Menu**: User reported when scrolling down, ContentReader text is visible all the way up to Profile tab (under the Main menu). User confirmed initial top border placement is correct, issue only occurs when scrolling. Investigation completed: Main menu positioned sticky with `top: calc(33vh + 3rem)` and `z-10` (PortfolioTab.tsx lines 549-550), ContentReader positioned with `marginTop: calc(45vh - 33vh - 3rem + 25px)` which positions it below Profile header initially (ContentReader.tsx line 67). Root cause identified: ContentReader is in normal document flow (not fixed/absolute) per browser scrollbar approach from Stage 2, so it scrolls with page. Main menu is sticky and stays in place. When page scrolls, ContentReader content scrolls up and becomes visible under the sticky Main menu. Issue: No constraint preventing ContentReader content from scrolling above Main menu's bottom edge. Expected behavior: ContentReader content should start below Main menu's bottom edge and remain below it during scrolling. Solution approach: Need to calculate Main menu height (MainMenu component + container padding + gap), add padding-top or adjust margin-top to ContentReader content to account for Main menu height, ensure content starts below Main menu bottom edge. Must preserve initial top border placement (user confirmed it's correct). **Documentation Updates**: Added Stage 5 "Fix Right Border Horizontal Scrolling Issue" to Step 4.1 (planning doc lines 1758-1775) with comprehensive bug description, investigation findings, root cause analysis, specific actions, testing checkpoint, and positioning guidance. Added Stage 6 "Fix Content Visible Under Main Menu When Scrolling" to Step 4.1 (planning doc lines 1777-1793) with comprehensive bug description, investigation findings, root cause analysis, specific actions, testing checkpoint, and positioning guidance. Both stages include detailed investigation findings documenting current implementation, specifications from logic doc and mockup, root cause analysis, expected behavior, and solution approaches. Result: Bug investigation complete, Stage 5 and Stage 6 added to Step 4.1, comprehensive documentation of both bugs with root cause analysis and solution approaches, ready for implementation when user approves.

Step 4.1 Stage 6 (Fix Content Visible Under Main Menu) – User requested implementing the approved sticky overlay mask without moving any ContentReader borders (portfolio-planning.md lines 1777-2169) so the reader keeps its verified placement while the menu hides overlapping text. – Added a viewport-aligned overlay to `components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx` lines 538-558: introduced `readerVisible`, `overlayTop`, and `overlayHeight` constants plus a conditional `<div aria-hidden="true">` that renders only when the reader is visible. The overlay uses `className="pointer-events-none fixed"` with `top: calc(33vh + 3rem)`, `height: calc(12vh + 3rem)`, `left: '50%'`, `transform: 'translateX(-50%)'`, and `width: 'min(1280px, calc(100vw - 4rem))'`, producing a mask that matches the Main menu footprint inside the existing 1280px shell. Styling retains the theme background (`#0f1419`) and `zIndex: 5`, so it sits between the menu (`z-10`) and ContentReader (default stacking) while leaving every ContentReader margin/padding unchanged and keeping browser-scroll behavior; reverting the fix only requires removing this localized block. – Testing: visited `http://localhost:3000/portfolio-test`, selected “Social Management → Short format → Social sidebar” to confirm the collapsed-reader state, scrolled through the article and verified the mask prevents text from appearing above the menu line; repeated after re-expanding the menu to ensure the overlay still tracks correctly; console remained clean and no additional scrollbars appeared. – Result: Stage 6 ✅ COMPLETE with masked viewport region preventing overlap while preserving all reader borders/placement; `read_lints components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx` reports no new issues and manual browser checks show the text now stops below the Main menu in both collapsed and expanded states, ready for user verification on `/portfolio-test`.

Step 4.1 Stage 6 (Fix Content Visible Under Main Menu) – Follow-up implementation after user confirmed the overlay violated layout requirements (portfolio-planning.md lines 1777-2169): removed the temporary fixed overlay block from `components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx` (lines 538-558) to restore the exact Main menu/Profile spacing, then added a dynamic clip mask inside `components/ContentReader.tsx` so the reader borders and placement never move while the text is clipped precisely at the sticky menu line. The new approach keeps ContentReader in normal flow but tracks the viewport on scroll: introduced `useRef`, `useState`, and `useEffect` to measure the container’s `getBoundingClientRect().top`, compute the menu bottom (`33vh + 3rem`, converted to pixels at runtime), and update `clipPath/WebkitClipPath` via `inset(${offset}px 0 0 0)`; the effect listens to `scroll` (passive) and `resize`, ensuring any viewport size still respects the mask. Scrolling is still controlled by the browser scrollbar per requirements (no overflow or margin changes), and all border math from logic doc lines 306-312 remains untouched. Testing: reloaded `http://localhost:3000/portfolio-test`, re-selected “Social Management → Short format → Social sidebar”, inspected both expanded-reader and collapsed-reader states while scrolling—verified the clip path grows only while content would otherwise overlap the Main menu; console stayed clean and no extra backgrounds appeared. Result: Stage 6 ✅ COMPLETE (replacement fix) with precise clipping tied to viewport scroll, zero layout shifts, and no additional overlays; `read_lints components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx components/ContentReader.tsx` report no issues, masking now honors the original borders exactly.

Step X.Y (Ad-hoc ContentReader Padding & Vertical Shift Test) — User request "Do it" to remove extra empty space above ContentReader text and then move the entire reader box 300px down — First change: updated components/ContentReader.tsx by changing the CONTENT_TOP_PADDING constant from '295px' to '0px' so the inner ContentReader text starts at the very top of its box while keeping all other positioning logic (margins, overlay band, scrolling) unchanged. Second change: updated components/ContentReader.tsx marginTop value from `calc(45vh - 33vh - 3rem - 257px)` to `calc(45vh - 33vh - 3rem - 257px + 300px)` so the whole ContentReader container is shifted 300px further down the page without reintroducing internal top padding. Result: Top padding inside ContentReader is now zero, and the entire ContentReader box starts 300px lower on the page; scrolling behavior is unchanged, and Bug #2 (text overlapping Main menu) now needs to be re-checked visually by the user with this new placement. 
Step X.Y (Main Menu Sticky Alignment to Profile Bottom – Option B Implementation) — User request "Only option B" to make the Main menu bar stick exactly under the Profile tab on all window sizes — Updated components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx to measure the real rendered height of the Profile header and use that value as the sticky top offset for the Main menu container: (1) imported useRef from React and added a new `profileRef` (`useRef<HTMLDivElement | null>(null)`) plus `profileHeight` state (`useState<number>(0)`) to track the measured height; (2) wrapped the `Profile` component in a `<div ref={profileRef}>` so the layout measurement can see its full height; (3) added a new `useEffect` that, when the component mounts, reads `element.getBoundingClientRect().height` from `profileRef.current` for an initial measurement, then attaches a `ResizeObserver` to that element and updates `profileHeight` whenever the wrapper’s height changes (Profile expand/collapse, text wrapping changes, window resizes, etc.), disconnecting the observer on cleanup; (4) changed the Main menu wrapper (the sticky left sidebar container) to use `style={{ top: profileHeight }}` instead of the previous `style={{ top: 'calc(33vh + 3rem)' }}`, keeping the `sticky self-start` classes but now aligning the sticky line directly to the measured bottom of the Profile header instead of a viewport-percentage position. Result: The Profile header remains sticky at the very top of the window as before, but the Main menu’s sticky position is now tied to the actual Profile height instead of `33vh`, so visually the menu should sit immediately under the Profile on all monitors and in both landscape and portrait orientations; `profileHeight` starts at 0 until the first measurement, after which it updates automatically as the Profile’s height changes. 
Step X.Y (Main Menu Sticky Alignment – Profile Sticky Restored, Measurement Moved Inside Profile) — Follow‑up to Option B implementation after user observed that the Profile header no longer behaved as expected — Adjusted the implementation so that the Profile component itself stays exactly as a sticky header at `top: 0`, while still reporting its height to `PortfolioTab` for menu alignment: (1) Updated `components/Profile.tsx` to accept an optional `onHeightChange?: (height: number) => void` prop and introduced a new `headerRef` (`useRef<HTMLElement | null>(null)`), attaching it directly to the `<header>` element that already has `sticky top-0 z-40 bg-[#0f1419]`; (2) Added a new `useEffect` inside `Profile` which, when `onHeightChange` is provided, reads `headerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect().height` for an initial measurement and then uses a `ResizeObserver` on the same header element to call `onHeightChange` whenever the sticky header's height changes (EXPAND/Collapse, text wrapping, window resize), disconnecting on cleanup; (3) Simplified `components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx` by removing the outer `<div ref={profileRef}>` wrapper and the local `ResizeObserver` effect, keeping only a `profileHeight` state and passing `onHeightChange={setProfileHeight}` to `<Profile />`; (4) Left the Main menu wrapper unchanged from the previous step, still using `style={{ top: profileHeight }}` with `sticky self-start`, so its sticky offset remains tied to the current measured height of the sticky Profile header. Result: The Profile header is once again directly responsible for its own stickiness (no extra wrapper around it), so it should behave like before — always stuck to the very top of the viewport — while the Main menu still uses the dynamically updated `profileHeight` to stick exactly under the Profile bottom on all window sizes; the menubar logic was not altered in this follow‑up, only the way the Profile height is measured.

InfoMenu Database Field Display Investigation & Fix ✅ COMPLETE – User reported InfoMenu fields that need to pull information from databases don't display this information, requested investigation and fix following recommended steps with documentation in development log – Added comprehensive debug logging to investigate database field display issue: Added debug logging in loadContent function lines 174-214 to log query execution and raw response data (console.log '🔍 [DEBUG] Loading content with joins...' before query, console.error '❌ [DEBUG] Query error:' on error, console.log '🔍 [DEBUG] Query response:' with dataCount and firstItem structure including byline_options link_options publication_name publication_date author_name source_link fields using JSON.stringify for readable output), added debug logging in transformContentItem function lines 264-290 to log raw data structure and extracted options (console.log '🔍 [DEBUG] Transform ContentItem - Raw data:' with id title byline_style link_style byline_options link_options publication_name publication_date author_name source_link using JSON.stringify, console.log '🔍 [DEBUG] Extracted options:' with bylineOption linkOption byline_style_text link_style_text using JSON.stringify), added debug logging in data loading useEffect lines 460-470 to log selectedContent metadata fields after state updates (console.log '🔍 [DEBUG] SelectedContent metadata fields:' with all 6 InfoMenu fields publication_name publication_date byline_style_text author_name link_style_text source_link using JSON.stringify). Root cause identified from console logs: Supabase returns byline_options and link_options as single objects (not arrays) for one-to-one foreign key relationships, but transformation code was using array indexing raw.byline_options?.[0] which returns undefined for objects, causing bylineOption and linkOption to be null and byline_style_text/link_style_text to be empty strings. Console evidence: Raw data showed "byline_options": {"id": "...", "option_text": "Written for:"} (single object), but extraction showed "bylineOption": null because [0] indexing doesn't work on objects. Fix implemented: Updated transformContentItem function lines 279-286 to handle both array and object cases for byline_options and link_options (Array.isArray check: if array use [0], if object use directly, if null/undefined use null), updated ContentItemRaw interface lines 61-62 to reflect that byline_options and link_options can be either single objects or arrays (union type: { id: string; option_text: string } | { id: string; option_text: string }[] | null), added comment explaining the fix "Fix: byline_options and link_options are returned as single objects, not arrays" and "Handle both array and object cases for compatibility". Implementation details: Fix handles Supabase behavior where one-to-one foreign key relationships return single objects instead of arrays, transformation now correctly extracts option_text from both array and object formats, TypeScript interface updated to match actual Supabase response structure, code maintains backward compatibility if Supabase behavior changes to return arrays in future. File statistics: PortfolioTab.tsx grew from 640 lines to 672 lines (32 new lines: debug logging, 8 lines fix implementation, 2 lines interface update). Testing executed: User confirmed fix successful after testing, InfoMenu now displays byline_style_text and link_style_text correctly (e.g. "Written for:", "Hosted by", "Read original", "View project"), all metadata fields (publication_name, publication_date, byline_style_text, author_name, link_style_text, source_link) now display correctly in InfoMenu component, debug logs confirmed extraction working (bylineOption and linkOption now contain option_text values instead of null). Potential errors to watch for during testing: Fix may not work if Supabase returns arrays in some cases (mitigated by Array.isArray check handling both cases), TypeScript may complain if union type not handled correctly (mitigated by explicit type checks), debug logs may show different structure if Supabase version changes (expected behavior fix handles both formats). Code quality verification: Fix follows defensive programming pattern (handles both expected formats), TypeScript interface accurately reflects actual data structure, code comments explain why fix is needed, transformation logic correctly extracts option_text from both array and object formats, no linter errors introduced. Result: Investigation ✅ COMPLETE with root cause identified (Supabase returns objects not arrays for one-to-one FK relationships), Fix ✅ COMPLETE with transformation logic updated to handle both array and object formats (lines 279-286), TypeScript interface updated to match actual structure (lines 61-62), Fix Verification ✅ COMPLETE with user confirming InfoMenu now displays all database fields correctly (byline_style_text and link_style_text extracted from byline_options and link_options tables, all 6 metadata fields displaying properly), Step 4.5 ✅ COMPLETE with InfoMenu component fully functional including database field display, ready for Step 4.6 Info Menu Metadata Display implementation

Added conditional rendering for Line 3 lines 78-99 (link style: Link to Original Source) with outer conditional `{(metadata.link_style_text || metadata.source_link) && (...)}` to hide line if both fields null/empty, inner conditionals display link_style_text in green if exists, show ": " separator only if both fields exist, if source_link exists render as clickable link with hover effect target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer", if source_link null/empty but link_style_text exists display "—" placeholder in gray, link element only renders if source_link is truthy (valid URL check). Step 5.5 Stage 1 (Create Helper Function for Collection Tab Content Selection) ✅ COMPLETE – User issued /stage-complete-proceed command after Step 5.5 planning complete, requested mark current stage complete in documentation update documentation abundantly with all edits mark all potential errors update development log proceed to next stage – Created collection tab content selection helper functions in PortfolioTab.tsx per planning doc Stage 1 specification: Added COLLECTION TAB CONTENT SELECTION HELPER FUNCTIONS section lines 461-530 after autoSelectInitialContent function (after line 459) per planning doc positioning. Created findCategorySubcategoryForContent helper function lines 464-477: Parameters `content: ContentItem categories: Category[] subcategories: Subcategory[]` returns `{ category: Category | null, subcategory: Subcategory | null }`, finds subcategory by `content.subcategory_id` using `subcategories.find(s => s.id === content.subcategory_id)`, finds category by `subcategory.category_id` using `categories.find(c => c.id === subcategory.category_id)`, handles edge case returns null if subcategory or category not found. Created autoSelectCollectionContent function lines 479-530: Parameters `filteredContent: ContentItem[] currentContent: ContentItem | null collectionSlug: string filteredCategories: Category[] filteredSubcategories: Subcategory[]` returns `{ selectedCategory: Category | null, selectedSubcategory: Subcategory | null, selectedContent: ContentItem | null }`, implements selection logic per logic doc lines 103-105. Edge case handling: Returns all null if filteredContent is empty (empty collection). Current content check: Checks if currentContent is in collection using `currentContent.collection_slugs.includes(collectionSlug)` (handles undefined/null arrays), if current content in collection keeps it selected and finds its category/subcategory using findCategorySubcategoryForContent, if current content NOT in collection selects first content by order_index (sorts filteredContent by order_index ascending takes first), finds category/subcategory for selected content using findCategorySubcategoryForContent. Step 5.5 Stage 2 (Create useEffect for Collection Tab Content Selection) ✅ COMPLETE – User issued /stage-complete-proceed command after Stage 1 testing successful, requested mark current stage complete in documentation update documentation abundantly with all edits mark all potential errors update development log proceed to next stage – Created useEffect hook for collection tab content selection in PortfolioTab.tsx per planning doc Stage 2 specification: Added COLLECTION TAB CONTENT SELECTION section lines 700-750 after data loading effects (after line 698 before debug logging) per planning doc positioning. Added useRef import line 3: Changed from `import { useState, useEffect, useMemo, useCallback } from 'react'` to `import { useState, useEffect, useMemo, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'` to support tracking previous activeTab. Created prevActiveTabRef useRef hook line 703: `const prevActiveTabRef = useRef<string>(activeTab)` to track previous activeTab value and only run auto-selection when tab actually changes. Created useEffect hook lines 705-750: Dependencies `[activeTab, activeCollections, filteredContent, filteredCategories, filteredSubcategories, selectedContent, loadingContent, loadingCategories]` per planning doc specification (removed selectedCategory selectedSubcategory from dependencies to prevent re-running on manual selection), implements auto-selection logic when collection tab becomes active. Guards and early returns: Returns early if main tab active using `if (!isCollectionTab(activeTab, activeCollections)) return`, checks if activeTab changed using `const activeTabChanged = prevActiveTabRef.current !== activeTab` and `prevActiveTabRef.current = activeTab`, returns early if activeTab didn't change using `if (!activeTabChanged) return` (prevents re-running when user manually selects categories/subcategories), returns early if still loading using `if (loadingContent || loadingCategories || filteredContent.length === 0) return`, returns early if collection slug not found using `if (!collectionSlug) return`. Selection logic: Gets collection slug using `getCurrentCollectionSlug(activeTab, activeCollections)`, calls `autoSelectCollectionContent` with filtered data and current selectedContent, updates state only if selection changed (prevents infinite loops) using comparison `selection.selectedContent?.id !== selectedContent?.id || selection.selectedCategory?.id !== selectedCategory?.id || selection.selectedSubcategory?.id !== selectedSubcategory?.id`, updates state using `setSelectedCategory setSelectedSubcategory setSelectedContent`, ensures pageState is expanded-reader using `if (selection.selectedContent) setPageState('expanded-reader')`. Bug fix: User reported "I can't select other categories and subcategories when a collection is selected. I can switch between different content items in the same subcategory" – Issue identified: useEffect had selectedCategory and selectedSubcategory in dependency array causing it to re-run whenever user manually selected category/subcategory, which then re-ran autoSelectCollectionContent and reset selection. Fix applied: Added useRef to track previous activeTab, only run auto-selection when activeTab actually changes (not on manual selection changes), removed selectedCategory and selectedSubcategory from dependency array. Result: User can now manually select categories and subcategories in collection tabs without auto-selection interfering. Implementation details: useEffect properly typed with TypeScript (correct dependency array), infinite loop prevention implemented correctly (only updates state if selection changed), activeTab change detection implemented correctly (useRef tracks previous value), edge cases handled correctly (loading states empty content main tab active), selection logic follows planning doc specification exactly, comments added explaining when selection happens and why, positioned correctly after data loading effects. File statistics: PortfolioTab.tsx grew from 904 lines to 950 lines (46 new lines: 1 useRef import, 1 section comment line, 1 prevActiveTabRef line, useEffect hook with comments totaling 43 lines), all code added after data loading effects per planning doc positioning. Testing executed: User confirmed testing successful after bug fix (useEffect created content auto-selects when collection tab opens current content kept if in collection first content selected if current not in collection category/subcategory updated correctly pageState set to expanded-reader no infinite loops user can manually select categories/subcategories in collection tabs no TypeScript errors), ran read_lints tool on components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx reporting no linter errors, TypeScript validates useEffect hook correctly (all dependencies correctly typed), TypeScript validates selection logic correctly (autoSelectCollectionContent called correctly state updates correct), no compilation errors, file compiles successfully, content selection fully functional, manual category/subcategory selection works correctly. Potential errors to watch for during Stage 3: Content may not auto-select if filteredContent not ready (mitigated by loading guards), infinite loops may occur if state comparison fails (mitigated by ID comparison before state updates), category/subcategory may not match if filtered arrays not updated (mitigated by including filteredCategories filteredSubcategories in dependencies), pageState may not update if selectedContent is null (mitigated by conditional setPageState), manual selection may be overridden if activeTab change detection fails (mitigated by useRef tracking). Code quality verification: useEffect follows planning doc Stage 2 specification exactly, all dependencies correctly included for reactive updates (selectedCategory selectedSubcategory removed to prevent interference), infinite loop prevention implemented correctly (ID comparison before state updates), activeTab change detection implemented correctly (useRef tracks previous value), edge cases handled correctly (loading states empty content main tab active), selection logic follows logic doc specification (lines 103-105), positioned correctly after data loading effects, comments added explaining when selection happens, bug fix documented, ready for Stage 3 Test Collection Tab Content Selection Behavior. Result: Stage 2 ✅ COMPLETE with components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx updated (950 lines total, grew from 904 lines), useEffect hook created for collection tab content selection, useRef added to track previous activeTab, activeTab change detection implemented, selectedCategory selectedSubcategory removed from dependencies, infinite loop prevention implemented, edge cases handled correctly, selection logic follows logic doc specification, bug fix applied (manual category/subcategory selection now works), no TypeScript errors, no linter errors, clean compilation, content auto-selects when collection tab becomes active, user can manually select categories/subcategories, ready for Stage 3 Test Collection Tab Content Selection Behavior implementation

Step 6.6 Stage 1 (Menu Bar Expanded State Visibility Issue - Strategy A Fix) ✅ COMPLETE ✅ VERIFIED SUCCESSFUL – User reported bug: "In expanded state, only the bottom two categories of Main Menu are visible. Top categories are cut off above the viewport" – User investigation revealed: `profileHeight: 0px` in console logs, Menu Bar using fallback `200px` value instead of actual Profile height, Menu Bar positioned too high causing top categories to be cut off. Root cause identified: Profile component's height measurement useEffect only depended on `[onHeightChange]`, didn't re-run when profile data loaded or when expanded/collapsed state changed, component returns `null` before profile data loads preventing `headerRef` from being attached, measurement never happened or happened before element rendered. Strategy A implemented: Fixed Profile height measurement timing in components/Profile.tsx (lines 46-70): Added `profile` and `isExpanded` to useEffect dependency array `[onHeightChange, profile, isExpanded]` so effect re-runs when profile data loads and when expand/collapse state changes, added early return check `if (!profile) return` to prevent measurement attempt before profile data available, wrapped initial measurement in `setTimeout(..., 0)` to ensure measurement happens after DOM fully rendered, added console.log for debugging when `headerRef.current` is null and when height is measured. Improved console logging in components/tabs/PortfolioTab.tsx (lines 756-759): Added warning when `profileHeight === 0` to identify when Profile height measurement is failing, improved fallback message to indicate Profile height not yet measured. User verification: User confirmed "Profile and Menu bar work as expected" – Profile height measurement now working correctly, Menu Bar positioned correctly below Profile with proper spacing, all categories visible in expanded state (not cut off), Menu Bar uses actual Profile height instead of fallback, expanded state layout functioning correctly. File statistics: Profile.tsx useEffect updated (added dependencies, early return, setTimeout, console logs), PortfolioTab.tsx console logging improved (warning for 0px, better fallback message), no other files modified. Documentation updated: Added implementation section to portfolio-layout-reset.md documenting Strategy A fix with before/after code, expected behavior, testing instructions, marked implementation as complete and verified successful. Result: Strategy A ✅ SUCCESSFUL with Profile height measurement timing fixed, useEffect now depends on profile data and expanded state, measurement happens after DOM renders, console logging improved for debugging, user verified Profile and Menu Bar working as expected, all categories visible in expanded state, Menu Bar positioned correctly, issue resolved

Testing executed: User verified testing successful - selecting entries triggers reordering, selected entry moves to top, relative order maintained, rapid selection doesn't cause race conditions. Code quality verification: reorderColumn called correctly for all three column types, animation handling prevents race conditions, render logic uses order arrays correctly, filtering and color coding maintained, visual reordering working correctly. Step 7.1 Stage 1 (Visual Reordering State Management) - Infinite Loop Bug Fix ✅ COMPLETE – User reported error: "Maximum update depth exceeded" infinite loop in MainMenu component – Error occurred in three locations: MainMenu.tsx line 103 (useEffect setting categoriesOrder), PortfolioTab.tsx line 874 (handleCategorySelect), PortfolioTab.tsx line 920 (handleContentSelect). Root cause identified: `sortedCategories` was being recreated on every render as `const sortedCategories = [...categories].sort(...)` creating new array reference each render, useEffect dependency on `sortedCategories` triggered on every render, state update in useEffect caused re-render, creating infinite loop. Additional issue: useEffect hooks were updating state unconditionally even when order arrays hadn't changed, causing unnecessary re-renders. Strategy implemented: Memoized sortedCategories using useMemo to prevent new array creation on every render (only recreates when categories prop changes), added conditional state updates in all three useEffect hooks to only update if order actually changed (compare array lengths and contents before updating), prevents infinite loops while maintaining correct initialization and syncing behavior. Code changes: MainMenu.tsx line 75-77 changed from `const sortedCategories = [...categories].sort(...)` to `useMemo(() => [...categories].sort(...), [categories])` memoizing sorted array, MainMenu.tsx lines 100-104 updated categoriesOrder useEffect to conditionally update only if order changed (compare lengths and contents), MainMenu.tsx lines 106-116 updated subcategoriesOrder and contentOrder useEffects with same conditional update logic. Result: Infinite loop resolved, sortedCategories only recreates when categories prop changes, order arrays only update when order actually changes, no unnecessary re-renders, state management working correctly. File statistics: MainMenu.tsx updated (memoized sortedCategories, added conditional updates to all three useEffect hooks), no other files modified. Testing executed: No infinite loop errors, order arrays initialize correctly, arrays update only when data changes, no unnecessary re-renders, TypeScript errors resolved. Code quality verification: sortedCategories properly memoized, useEffect hooks have proper conditional updates, infinite loop prevention working correctly, state management stable. Result: ✅ COMPLETE with infinite loop bug fixed, sortedCategories memoized, conditional state updates prevent unnecessary re-renders, Stage 1 state management working correctly
